drivers.name: material_cost

drivers.name: support_material
drivers.source: support structure 0.2×net = 0.0043 kg powder printed then removed (INCLUDED in material_cost above; surfaced for inspection) [metal-AM assumption, not shop-validated]

drivers.source: 0.2912 hr × $180/hr × region-labor ×1  [build-job 325mm ÷ 6mm/hr = 54.2hr full build ÷ 186 parts/build (packing 0.08, env (250, 250, 325)) = 0.291hr/part]

drivers.name: stress_relief_cost

routing.reasoning: Compact prismatic solid (91% of bbox filled, 2.0:1 aspect) in metal → machine from billet (CNC 3-/5-axis).

notes: DFM note: processes forging, investment_casting, sand_casting are costed but flagged NOT DFM-ready as-modeled (the engine reports ERROR-level blockers — these parts were modeled for 3D printing, so molding/casting lack draft). Their cost shows the tooling economics *if* the part is redesigned for that process. Set strict_dfm=True to exclude them entirely.
Geometric routing: this part reads as a 'prismatic_block' → cnc_3axis. Compact prismatic solid (91% of bbox filled, 2.0:1 aspect) in metal → machine from billet (CNC 3-/5-axis).
Absolute cost is ±40–60% (cycle-time/tooling defaults). The crossover quantity and make-vs-buy direction are robust to it because they depend on the fixed-vs-variable split, driven by your rates.

assumptions.name: stock_allowance
assumptions.source: CNC billet oversize (milling: on bounding box; turning: on hull)

assumptions.name: machine_labor_frac
assumptions.source: operator-labor share of the machine rate that scales with region (capital/facility stays global) [assumption, not shop-validated]

assumptions.name: perishable_frac
assumptions.source: perishable tooling/consumables as a fraction of CNC machine cost [assumption, not shop-validated]

assumptions.name: complexity
assumptions.source: tooling complexity = moderate (DEFAULT moderate; raise for slides/side-actions)

decision.note: Make by cnc_3axis (API 13Cr) — $22.98/unit at qty 50, the cheapest make-as-is option and your low-volume pick. cnc_3axis is cheapest at every quantity tested — no tooling crossover.

verification.note: Machine fit is a MEASURED-geometry × USER-declared-capability comparison. 'unknown' when no inventory is declared or a required capability is undeclared — never a fabricated pass. Environment exclusions cite the material property/standard. Known limitations: the 5-axis/undercut need is inherited from the upstream process router (not re-derived from geometry); force gates (tonnage/taper) require a declared force.

identity.reason: no org corpus yet — falls back to standard-catalog + file metadata (never a fabricated identity)
caveats: a retrieved SUGGESTION to confirm, not a verified identity; retrieval can be wrong — check before trusting.
geometry_similarity is a documented proxy (exp(-z_scored_L2 / 2.0)), monotonic in geometric closeness — NOT a probability.
identity.provenance: RETRIEVED (org corpus: geometry k-NN + name match)
